compiled_state.title: Add preplanning.interactive config key to enable/disable interactive mode
compiled_state.author: Claude
compiled_state.description:
## Context
Automation agents and pipelines that invoke preplanning programmatically — within sprint, CI workflows, or multi-agent orchestration — are blocked today because preplanning pauses at six checkpoints waiting for human input: epic selection, escalation policy, scope clarification, reconciliation approval, story dashboard review, and final story map approval. When no operator is present the skill stalls indefinitely and automation fails. The DSO plugin itself needs a configuration-backed mechanism to guarantee non-interactive execution in automated contexts.

## Success Criteria
- A `preplanning.interactive` boolean key (absent or `false` = non-interactive; `true` = interactive) is read at preplanning startup via `read-config.sh` and governs all interactive checkpoints
- In non-interactive mode, all interactive checkpoints have defined non-blocking behavior: invocation without an epic-id exits immediately with a clear error message; scope clarification needed during reconciliation exits immediately with a clear error message identifying what needs clarification; reconciliation approval for existing children is auto-applied; escalation policy defaults to "Escalate when blocked" without prompting; story dashboard is suppressed; final story map approval is skipped; ui-designer dispatch protocol (Phase 4 Step 6) uses INTERACTIVITY_DEFERRED paths
- `CONFIGURATION-REFERENCE.md` and `workflow-config-schema.json` document `preplanning.interactive` with description, accepted values (true/false), default (non-interactive when absent), per-checkpoint behavior table, and a migration note for existing projects upgrading from always-interactive behavior
- `/dso:onboarding` asks whether the project should use interactive preplanning and writes `preplanning.interactive` to the generated config accordingly
- This project's `.claude/dso-config.conf` sets `preplanning.interactive=false` explicitly
- An automated preplanning invocation run with `preplanning.interactive` absent or `false` and a valid epic-id completes end-to-end without pausing on any checkpoint — producing a story map — confirming the automation-blocking behavior is resolved; tests exercising all six non-interactive checkpoint fallbacks pass

## Done Definitions
- When this epic is complete, preplanning runs end-to-end without blocking on any checkpoint when `preplanning.interactive` is absent or false, producing a story map or a structured error exit
- When this epic is complete, `preplanning.interactive=true` preserves today's interactive behavior at all six checkpoints unchanged
- When this epic is complete, `CONFIGURATION-REFERENCE.md` and `workflow-config-schema.json` document the key with per-checkpoint behavior table and a migration note for existing projects
- When this epic is complete, `/dso:onboarding` writes `preplanning.interactive` to generated project configs based on the operator's preference
- When this epic is complete, this project's `.claude/dso-config.conf` has `preplanning.interactive=false` explicitly set and automated preplanning tests pass

## Scope
- IN: `plugins/dso/skills/preplanning/SKILL.md` conditional branches at all 6+1 interactive checkpoints; `plugins/dso/docs/CONFIGURATION-REFERENCE.md` new key entry; `plugins/dso/docs/workflow-config-schema.json` schema addition; `plugins/dso/skills/onboarding/SKILL.md` probe question; `plugins/dso/skills/preplanning/prompts/ui-designer-dispatch-protocol.md` PREPLANNING_INTERACTIVE wiring; `.claude/dso-config.conf` key update; tests for all 6 non-interactive fallback behaviors
- OUT: Non-interactive mode for other skills (sprint, debug-everything, fix-bug); global interactivity config affecting multiple skills; changes to interactive mode behavior (today's behavior unchanged when `preplanning.interactive=true`)

## Considerations
- [Testing] Preplanning SKILL.md modification — tests must cover all 6 non-interactive fallback behaviors including the Delete-in-reconciliation edge case
- [Reliability] Auto-approval of reconciliation in non-interactive mode applies unconditionally including Delete recommendations — guard against closing in-progress stories (see Scenario Analysis)
- [Maintainability] PREPLANNING_INTERACTIVE flag accessed at 6–7 checkpoints — read config once at startup, pass as parameter; avoid re-reading at each checkpoint

## Scenario Analysis
- **Absent key / empty string** (high, configuration): `read-config.sh` returns empty string for absent keys — implementation must use `[[ "$PREPLANNING_INTERACTIVE" == "true" ]]`, not `!= "false"`
- **Case-sensitive boolean** (medium, configuration): `preplanning.interactive=False` (capital F) silently becomes non-interactive — implementation should normalize value to lowercase
- **Auto-approval includes Delete** (high, runtime): reconciliation auto-approval in non-interactive mode applies unconditionally including Delete recommendations — may close in-progress stories without human review; implementation should guard or restrict scope
- **Breaking change for existing users** (high, configuration): absent key = non-interactive is a silent behavior change for all current projects on upgrade — migration note in docs and release notes required
- **Schema not updated** (medium, deployment): `workflow-config-schema.json` must be updated alongside the markdown doc
- **UI designer dispatch not wired** (high, runtime): Phase 4 Step 6 dispatch protocol must explicitly use PREPLANNING_INTERACTIVE to select between interactive and INTERACTIVITY_DEFERRED paths
- **Unstructured exit signals** (medium, configuration): exit-immediately paths should use distinct exit codes so CI can distinguish "missing epic-id" from "scope clarification needed"

## Dependencies
None

## Approach
Add `preplanning.interactive` to the config schema and `workflow-config-schema.json`. Read the value once at preplanning startup via `read-config.sh` into a local `PREPLANNING_INTERACTIVE` flag, normalizing to lowercase. Add a conditional branch at each of the six interactive checkpoints (plus wire the ui-designer dispatch protocol's interactivity mode selection). Update `CONFIGURATION-REFERENCE.md` with the new key entry including a per-checkpoint behavior table and migration note. Add a probe question to `/dso:onboarding` and update config generation. Set `preplanning.interactive=false` in this project's `.claude/dso-config.conf`.

### Planning Intelligence Log
- **Web research (Step 2.6)**: not triggered — Bright-line conditions that fired: none
- **Scenario analysis (Step 2.75)**: triggered — Scenarios surviving blue team filter: 7 of 10
- **Practitioner-requested additional cycles**: none
- **Follow-on scrutiny (Step 0)**: not triggered
- **Feasibility resolution (Step 2.5)**: not triggered
- **LLM-instruction signal (Step 5)**: not triggered
